data.title: Marex Group announces pricing of public offering of 8.47M ordinary shares
data.description:
• Marex Group (NASDAQ:MRX) on Thursday announced the pricing of the public offering of 8.47M ordinary shares by certain selling shareholders at $24.00 per share.
  • In connection with the offering, the selling shareholders have granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 1.27M ordinary shares.
  • Marex is not selling any ordinary shares in the offering and will not receive any proceeds.
  • The offering is expected to close on October 25.
  • MRX is +1.82% to $25.15.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Tesla boosts Nasdaq; S&P gives up gains to turn red, while IBM, Honeywell weigh on Dow
data.description:
Wall Street's three major averages were mixed on Thursday. A post-earnings surge in Tesla (TSLA) propped up the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND), but that was countered by a slide in IBM (IBM) and Honeywell (HON) which weighed on the blue-chip Dow (DJI).

Meanwhile, a relentless bond sell-off that started this week partly due to a recalibration of Federal Reserve rate cut expectations finally stalled.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) opened higher and held on to small gains until about midday when it turned negative. It was last -0.15% at 5,790.59 points, while the Dow (DJI) was -0.70% at 42,216.02 points. The Nasdaq (COMP:IND) was +0.25% at 18,322.85 points.

Of the 11 sectors, nine were in the red.

Tesla (TSLA) was among the top percentage gainers on both the S&P and the Nasdaq. The electric vehicle maker's stock soared +18.3%, after the company posted one of its strongest quarterly reports in a while and top boss Elon Musk projected as much as 30% deliveries growth in 2025.

However, the positive sentiment around Tesla (TSLA) failed to carry over to its Magnificent 7 peers, as each member of the club other than Amazon (AMZN) and Alphabet (GOOG)(GOOGL) slipped marginally.

Some of the gains from Tesla (TSLA) were offset by a -7% slump in IBM (IBM). The legacy tech firm reported a quarterly top-line miss, as a solid increase in revenue in its software business was offset by flat sales in its consulting segment and a revenue dip in its infrastructure unit.

IBM (IBM) was the biggest drag on the blue-chip Dow (DJI), followed by Honeywell (HON). The industrial conglomerate shed -4.5% after cutting its full-year revenue guidance.

A -2.1% dip in Boeing (BA) also put pressure on the Dow (DJI). The troubled planemaker a day ago disclosed a whopping $6B in quarterly losses due to a crippling machinists' strike. Then, late Wednesday, in another blow to the company, the factory workers voted to reject a new contract offer from the firm that would have ended a six-week strike.

In other earnings-related moves, UPS (UPS) was +4.4%, after the parcel delivery giant and economic bellwether returned to revenue and profit growth after nearly two years.

Turning to the fixed-income markets, U.S. Treasury yields fell as a weekly bond sell-off lost steam. Some of that selling had been attributed to traders steadily recalibrating their expectations for aggressive Fed rate cuts following strong economic data recently. Comments from well-known Fed speakers this week have expressed a preference for gradual policy easing.

The benchmark 10-year yield (US10Y) was down 4 basis points to 4.20%, while the shorter-end 2-year yield (US2Y) was also down 4 basis points to 4.05%.

For more, see how Treasury yields have done across the curve on the Seeking Alpha bond page.

Thursday's economic calendar was fairly busy. Before the opening bell, data showed the number of Americans filing for initial jobless claims in the past week unexpectedly fell to 227K.

"It doesn't take much fancy analysis to see that today's initial jobless claims number was much better than expected (227K vs 242K consensus), particularly given the impact of hurricanes and strikes / furloughs. Here's my fancy analysis anyway to get a pulse check on just how much better than expected claims were: the aforementioned transitory factors still boosted initial jobless claims by ~12k during the week ending Oct 19, down from ~18k the week before," Parker Ross, global chief economist at Arch Capital Group, said on X (formerly Twitter).

"Thus, today's initial claims data was one of the best prints of the year when accounting for these recent one-offs," Ross added.

Shortly after the start of regular trading, S&P Global reported an improvement in Flash U.S. Composite PMI in October, signaling a "sustained solid expansion of business activity at the start of the fourth quarter."

That was followed by the U.S. Census Bureau's update on U.S. new home sales, which powered past estimates in September.

More on the markets

  • Let's Start Worrying About Rising Bond Yields
  • S&P Equal Weight Vs. Cap Weight
  • Turnaround Tuesday Comes Late
  • Bond market 'sniffing out Trump win' contributes to 10-year yield visiting 3-month high
  • SP500: Bracing For Turbulence

data.title: Old Republic stock dips after Q3 earnings slip as combined ratio rises
data.description:
Old Republic International (NYSE:ORI) stock drifted down 2.6% in Thursday afternoon trading after the insurer turned in Q3 earnings that retreated both sequentially and from a year before as its combined ratio increased.

Q2 EPS, excluding investment gains/losses, of $0.71, slightly surpassing the $0.70 average analyst estimate, fell from $0.76 in Q2 and $0.72 in Q3 2023.

Total operating revenue of $2.14B, topping the $2.07B consensus, advanced from $2.01B in the prior quarter and $1.95B in Q3 of last year.

Net premiums and fees earned totaled $1.93B, up from $1.80B in Q2 and $1.76B in the year-earlier quarter.

Net investment income rose to $171.0M from $167.4M in Q2 and $145.9M a year ago, driven by higher investment yields.

Combined ratio of 95.0% compared with 93.5% in Q2 and 91.9% in Q3 2023, reflecting lower levels of favorable loss reserve development.

Book value per share was $25.71 at Sept. 30, up from $23.31 at Dec. 31, 2023.

Conference call at 3:00 p.m. ET.

data.title: Molina stock rallies 23% after Q3 earnings report beats Street
data.description:
Molina Healthcare (NYSE:MOH) shares rallied 23% Thursday, the day after the company released a Q3 earnings report that beat Street estimates on both the top and bottom lines.

Late Wednesday, Molina (MOH) reported a Q3 adjusted profit of $6.01 per share on revenue of $10.34B, with premium revenue climbing 18% year-over-year to $9.69B. Analysts, on average, had been expecting adjusted EPS of $5.94 on revenue of $9.91B.

Molina (MOH) attributed the premium revenue increase to new contract wins, acquisitions and growth in its current footprint, which was partially offset by the impact of Medicaid redeterminations, according to a statement.

The company added that its consolidated medical care ratio for Q3 was 89.2%, which it said reflected a continued focus on managing medical costs.

Molina (MOH) also reaffirmed its 2024 guidance of premium revenue of approximately $38B and adjusted EPS of at least $23.50.

“Continued strong performance due to Marketplace, operating leverage, and higher net investment income are expected to offset the higher-than-expected trend in Medicaid and Medicare in the second half of the year,” Molina (MOH) added.

data.title: D.E. Shaw supports Mantle Ridge proxy fight with Air Products - CNBC
data.description:
Hedge fund D.E. Shaw supports activist investor Mantle Ridge's efforts to get board seats at Air Products (ADP).

D.E. Shaw will not be filing a slate of its own candidate for Air Products (NYSE:APD), CNBC's David Faber reported earlier on Thursday, citing people familiar with the matter. The nominating window for the APD board closes on Sunday.

Mantle Ridge has offered nominees for all nine of Air Products (APD) board seats, according to Faber.

The update comes after it was reported earlier this month that D.E. Shaw had taken a stake in the gas maker and was pushing for changes, including new board directors. D.E. Shaw accumulated a $1 billion economic stake in Air Products, some of which is likely through swaps, and wants three board seats, Faber said at the time.

Mantle Ridge, which is helmed by Paul Hilal, is expected to push the industrial gas firm on succession planning for CEO Seifi Ghasemi as well as for improvements to the company's strategy and capital allocation. Air Products jumped 9.5% on Oct. 7 after a report that Mantle Ridge has accumulated a more than $1 billion stake in the industrial gas maker.

Two former top executives at Linde Plc (LIN) are joining with Mantle Ridge as the activist investor pushes for changes at Air Products (APD), Reuters reported last Tuesday. Dennis Reilley and Eduardo Menezes are teaming up with Mantle Ridge.

Mantle Ridge's Hilal is eyeing Reilley to become Air Products (APD) executive chairman, while Menezes may become the gas maker's CEO, Reuters said.

"Our Call: Mantle Ridge has added two former Linde Executives (Dennis Reilly and Eduardo Menezes) to reshape APD's sustainability growth strategy. This "Dream Team" could make a difference in the battle for control given strong track records and experience," Wells Fargo analyst Michael Sison wrote in a note last week.

data.title: This is what really caused the US10Y to rise – analyst
data.description:
The rise in the 10-year yield (US10Y) has nothing to do with the Federal Reserve policy rate expectation, by a re-pricing of the Fed’s rate-cutting path after strong non-farm payrolls in September and a rise in GDP for the third quarter, said Michael Darda, chief economist and market strategist at Roth MKM, in a note.

The 10-year Treasury note yield (US10Y) was up more than 60 basis points from the year-to-date lows seen Sept. 16, as of yesterday.

Since that date, only 40% of the jump in rates has been driven by higher inflation expectations, and 60% has been driven by real rates, said Darda.

From the Sept. 16 low, the 10-year yield (US10Y) bottomed at 3.62%, real rates rose 37 basis points, and the inflation expectation rose 25 basis points.

“This move has been associated with a 70 basis points rise in terminal Fed policy rate expectations,” Darda said, and argued that the association is a misread.

“The widespread view is that the spike in rates is due to some combination of a bond market repudiation of the Fed’s rate cut path and rising risk of a post-election inflation shock (due to tariffs and a sudden reversal of immigration flows).”

Darda said that the argument that the Fed distorted the Treasury market “doesn’t jibe with the data.”

He said that, at 17.4% of the current stock of marketable Treasury debt, the Fed’s presence in the public debt market “is no larger than it was in the early 2000s and smaller than it was during the mid-1970s.”

He added that, “although the rise in yields does correspond to Trump’s rise in the online betting markets, we believe most of the move has been driven by a re-pricing of the Fed’s rate-cutting path in the wake of a strong September non-farm payrolls report and a steady rise in GDP tracking estimates for Q3.”

Darda also said the is inclined to lean into defensive stock groups that have underperformed or sold off as the Treasury rates rose. Those include health care (XLV), consumer staples (XLP), and REITs (XLRE).

“Since rates bottomed out in Sep. 16, the healthcare sector (XLV) has been the worst-performing sector, down 4.3%,” he said. “Yet, the sector offers defensive characteristics, isn’t overly rate-sensitive, and screens well against normalized earnings estimates.”

The energy sector (XLE), on the other hand, is the third-best performer since that date, up 4.7%.

“Unlike the technology sector (XLK), which trades at nosebleed ratios to earnings, sales, and even liquidity, the energy sector (XLE) is the most undervalued sector of the S&P 500 (SP500) on a normalized basis.”

The U.S. 10-year yield (US10Y) is now at 4.21%, down 0.9% on the day.

data.title: Solventum gains amid report it's exploring sale of purification, filtration business
data.description:
Solventum (NYSE:SOLV) rose 4% after a report that it has hired bankers to explore to a sale of its purification and filtration business.

The sales process is in its early stages and there's a possibility no sale will happen, according to a WSJ report on Thursday, which cited people familiar with the matter.

Solventum (SOLV) would likely use proceeds from sale to pay down debt or for research and development.

Developing story ...

data.title: Northern Star Resources reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Northern Star Resources press release (OTCPK:NESRF): Q1 revenue of A$1,346M.
  • Net cash of A$148M at 30 September.
  • FY25 growth capital guidance between A$950 and A$1,020M.

data.title: Ladder Capital stock rises as NII helps fuel Q3 earnings beat
data.description:
Ladder Capital (NYSE:LADR) stock gained 2.8% in late morning trading on Thursday after Q3 earnings solidly topped the Wall Street consensus as its net interest income climbed from the prior quarter. Its undepreciated book value increased, and its provision for loan loss reserves declined.

Q3 distributable EPS of $0.30, beating the average analyst estimate of $0.27, edged down from $0.31 in Q2 and $0.31 in last year's Q3.

Q3 net interest income of $38.4M, vs. the Visible Alpha consensus of $31.2M, increased from $34.3M in the previous quarter and dipped from $38.8M a year ago.

Undepreciated book value per share of $13.81 at Sept. 30, 2024, rose from $13.71 at June 30.

Provision for loan loss reserves of $3.1M vs. $5.1M in Q2 and $7.5M in Q3 2023.

Real estate operating income of $25.3M dropped from $26.1M in the prior quarter and increased from $24.8M a year ago.

Total costs and operating expenses slipped to $39.4M from $40.6M in Q2 and rose from $37.9M in last year's Q3.

The company funded $3M of advances on previously originated loans and received $492M of balance sheet loan repayments.

It sold one REO property for $12M of net proceeds, generating a $0.3M net gain.

After Q3 ended, Ladder (NYSE:LADR) originated a $24M first mortgage loan secured by a multifamily property in Phoenix.

Cash and cash equivalents of $1.61B surged from $1.20B at June 30.

Earlier, Ladder Capital Non-GAAP EPS of $0.30 beats by $0.03, revenue of $70.97M beats by $8.01M

data.title: TKO signs over majority control to Endeavor to bolster portfolio with PBR, On Location, and IMG assets (update)
